title: solanum aviculare var. vescum
intro: Solanum aviculare var. vescum, also known as the eggplant or ground cherry, is a type of fruit-bearing plant in the Solanaceae family, commonly found in New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Solanum aviculare var. vescum include lowering blood sugar levels, reducing the risk of heart disease, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw or cooked as a vegetable.
